_note: Per-lesson role-play scenario. Grounded in A2/001 dialogue + vocab. Learner plays Anna; computer plays Tom. Each NPC node offers 2-3 branches so the same lesson language can be reached different ways. Consumed by RP.Engine + RP.classifiers.local (see roleplay/).
title: 001 · Summer Plans

npc.text: What are you going to do this summer, Anna?

routes.meaning: Anna says she is going to visit her grandparents in the countryside.
examples: I'm going to visit my grandparents in the countryside.
I am going to visit my grandparents.
I'll visit my grandparents this summer.
I'm going to the countryside to see my grandparents.
routes.hint: Say your plan: “I'm going to visit my grandparents in the countryside.”

routes.meaning: Anna names a different summer plan.
examples: I'm going to stay home and read.
I am going to the beach.
I'm going to a summer camp too.
I will travel with my family.
routes.hint: You can share any plan, or say “I'm going to visit my grandparents in the countryside.”

partial.message: Tell Tom your summer plan: “I'm going to visit my grandparents in the countryside.”

off_topic.message: Tom asked about your summer. Try “I'm going to visit my grandparents in the countryside.”

npc.text: Oh, nice! Are you going to visit family this summer too?

routes.meaning: Anna says yes, she will visit her grandparents in the countryside.
examples: Yes, I'm going to visit my grandparents in the countryside.
Yes, I'll visit my grandparents.
Yes, I am going to see my grandpa.
Yes, I'm going to the countryside.
routes.hint: Say yes and where: “Yes, I'm going to visit my grandparents in the countryside.”

partial.message: Tell Tom who you will visit: “I'm going to visit my grandparents in the countryside.”

off_topic.message: Tom asked about family. Try “Yes, I'm going to visit my grandparents in the countryside.”

npc.text: That sounds relaxing. How long will you stay?

routes.meaning: Anna says two weeks and that she will help her grandpa on the farm.
examples: For two weeks. I'll help my grandpa on the farm.
Two weeks. I will help my grandpa on the farm.
For two weeks. I'm going to help on the farm.
About two weeks, and I'll help my grandpa.
routes.hint: Say how long and what you'll do: “For two weeks. I'll help my grandpa on the farm.”

routes.meaning: Anna gives a different length of stay.
examples: Just for a few days.
For one week.
For a whole month!
Maybe ten days.
routes.hint: You can name any time, or say “For two weeks. I'll help my grandpa on the farm.”

partial.message: Tell Tom how long: “For two weeks. I'll help my grandpa on the farm.”

off_topic.message: Tom asked how long you will stay. Try “For two weeks. I'll help my grandpa on the farm.”

npc.text: Sounds fun, but also hard work!

routes.meaning: Anna agrees it is work but says she enjoys it, then asks Tom back.
examples: It is, but I enjoy it. What about you?
It is, but I like it. What about you?
Yes, but I enjoy it. And you?
It's hard, but fun. What about you?
routes.hint: Share your feeling and ask back: “It is, but I enjoy it. What about you?”

routes.meaning: Anna agrees it is hard work but stays positive.
examples: Yes, but I like helping.
It is hard, but I don't mind.
True, but the farm is fun.
Yes, but I love the countryside.
routes.hint: You can say “It is, but I enjoy it. What about you?”

partial.message: Tell Tom how you feel and ask back: “It is, but I enjoy it. What about you?”

off_topic.message: Tom said it's hard work. Try “It is, but I enjoy it. What about you?”

npc.text: I'm going to a summer camp because I want to make new friends.

routes.meaning: Anna says it is a great idea and asks what he will do there.
examples: That's a great idea. What will you do there?
That is a great idea! What will you do there?
Nice! What will you do at the camp?
Cool! What will you do there?
routes.hint: React and ask: “That's a great idea. What will you do there?”

routes.meaning: Anna asks where the camp is.
examples: That sounds fun! Where is the camp?
Where is your summer camp?
Nice! Where is it?
Where will the camp be?
routes.hint: You can ask “Where is the camp?” or say “That's a great idea. What will you do there?”

partial.message: React to Tom's plan: “That's a great idea. What will you do there?”

off_topic.message: Tom is going to camp. Try “That's a great idea. What will you do there?”

npc.text: It's near the mountains. We'll do lots of fun things.

routes.meaning: Anna asks what Tom will do there.
examples: What will you do there?
What will you do at the camp?
Wow! What will you do there?
That's cool. What will you do?
routes.hint: Ask about it: “What will you do there?”

partial.message: Ask Tom about the camp: “What will you do there?”

off_topic.message: Ask what Tom will do. Try “What will you do there?”

npc.text: We'll go hiking and swimming every day.

routes.meaning: Anna hopes they both have a wonderful summer.
examples: I hope we both have a wonderful summer!
I hope we both have a great summer!
That sounds amazing! Have a wonderful summer.
I hope you have a wonderful summer!
routes.hint: End warmly: “I hope we both have a wonderful summer!”

routes.meaning: Anna says the camp sounds great and wishes Tom fun.
examples: That sounds wonderful! Have fun.
Hiking and swimming sound great!
Wow, that will be so much fun!
That's amazing! Enjoy the camp.
routes.hint: You can say “I hope we both have a wonderful summer!”

partial.message: End the chat: “I hope we both have a wonderful summer!”

off_topic.message: Time to finish. Try “I hope we both have a wonderful summer!”

npc.text: Thanks, Anna! Have a great summer too. See you in September!
